steps.title: Identify the causal signal
steps.content: The passage uses since to explain why excavation is [blank]: "weeks or even months of hard, physically tiring work." This sustained physical effort is the key.

steps.title: Match the meaning to the choices
steps.content:
The blank needs to mean hard or demanding — "weeks or even months of hard, physically tiring work" is the explicit justification. Substitute each choice:
  • Choice A — cheap: means low in cost — the passage says nothing about the expense of excavation, only the physical effort required. ✗
  • Choice B — rapid: means fast — directly contradicts "weeks or even months"; the process is notably slow, not quick. ✗
  • Choice C — difficult: means hard or demanding — sustained, physically tiring work spanning weeks or months is precisely what makes a process difficult. ✓
  • Choice D — common: means frequently occurring or ordinary — the passage gives no information about how often basilosaur excavations happen. ✗
That matches choice C — C is correct.
